label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Anthony could have gone to the bookstore. The question gives us several time intervals when Anthony was doing other activities. We need to find the time window when he wasn't doing any of those things, and the bookstore was still open. Let me list out what I know: 1. Anthony woke up at 7am. This just tells me when he started his day, but not necessarily when he was doing specific activities. 2. David saw Anthony working at the office from 7am to 8am. So Anthony was at the office during that hour. 3. Leslie saw Anthony getting a coffee at the cafe from 8am to 11am. So Anthony was at the cafe for three hours, from 8am to 11am. 4. Sarah saw Anthony watching a movie at the theater from 1pm to 9pm. That's a long movie! Anthony was at the theater from 1pm to 9pm. 5. The bookstore was closed after 9pm. So Anthony must have gone to the bookstore before 9pm. Now, let me create a timeline of Anthony's known activities: - 7am to 8am: At the office (with David) - 8am to 11am: At the cafe (with Leslie) - 1pm to 9pm: At the theater (with Sarah) The bookstore was closed after 9pm, so Anthony had to go before that. Let me see what times are unaccounted for: - From 7am to 8am: At the office - From 8am to 11am: At the cafe - There's a gap between 11am and 1pm. Let me check if Anthony was doing anything during that time. According to the given information, he wasn't seen doing anything during this window. - From 1pm to 9pm: At the theater So the only time window when Anthony wasn't seen doing anything is from 11am to 1pm. During this time, the bookstore was still open (it's closed after 9pm), so Anthony could have gone to the bookstore during this window. Let me check the answer options: (A) 11am to 1pm → This matches my conclusion. (B) 8am to 11am → Anthony was at the cafe during this time.
